content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

July 18, 1989


Chris Reynolds
Field Representative
Senator Rebecca O. Morgan
830 Menlo Ave., Suite 200
Menlo Park, CA  94025

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-89-373

Dear Ms. Reynolds:

We have received your letter (copy enclosed) seeking written
confirmation of the advice provided to you during our telephone
conversation of June 13, 1989.

This is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects cur-
rent law and the advice I provided you.

If you have any further questions regarding this matter, do
not hesitate to contact me at (916) 322-5901.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

[illegible]

By: Blanca M. Breeze
Counsel, Legal Division

KED:BMB:plh

Enclosure


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

Senate
California Legislature

JUN 21 1989

COMMITTEES:
CHAIR:

SELECT COMMITTEE ON
INFANT AND CHILD CARE AND
DEVELOPMENT

REPLY TO:
STATE SENATOR
SACRAMENTO OFFICE
STATE CAPITOL
SACRAMENTO, CA 95814
(916) 445-6747

VICE CHAIR:

EDUCATION

MEMBER:

BUDGET AND FISCAL REVIEW

REBECCA Q. MORGAN

ENERGY AND PUBLIC
UTILITIES

ELEVENTH DISTRICT

REVENUE AND TAXATION
TRANSPORTATION

■

DISTRICT OFFICE
830 MENLO AVENUE
SUITE 200
MENLO PARK, CA 94025
(415) 666-6330
(408) 277-9624

JOINT COMMITTEE ON
SCIENCE AND TECHNOLOGY
JOINT COMMITTEE FOR
REVIEW OF THE MASTER
PLAN FOR HIGHER EDUCATION
SELECT COMMITTEE ON
SUBSTANCE ABUSE

Blanca Breeze
FPPC, Legal Division
P.O. Box 807
Sacramento, CA 95804


Dear Ms. Breeze,
This letter is to confirm our conversation
of June 13, 1989. As per our conversation, I understand:

1) The provisions of Proposition 73 are not violated when a
mass mailing exceeding 200 pieces per month is paid for (inclu-
ding all production and mailing fees) with contributions
raised after January 1, 1989 that have been identified and
deposited in an account that specifies the office for which
the funds are to be used, in this case: Morgan for Senate '92,
ID# 890213.

2) It is allowable to include a pre-printed request card for
voter registration materials in this mailing, which the recipient
may use to request a voter registration form from their
County Registrar of Voters.

I would appreciate it if you would confirm my understanding of
our conversation by writing me at: 830 Menlo Ave. #200, Menlo
Park, CA 94025.

Thank you,

Chris Reynolds
Field Representative
Senator Rebecca Q. Morgan

California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

June 22, 1989

Chris Reynolds
Field Representative
Senator Rebecca Q. Morgan
State Capitol
Sacramento, CA  95814

Re: Letter No. 89-373

Dear Mr. Reynolds:

We received your letter requesting confirmation of advice
under the Political Reform Act on June 21, 1989. Your letter has
been assigned to Blanca Breeze for response. If you have any
questions, you may contact her directly at (916) 322-5901.

If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without further
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. A confirming
response will be released after-it has gone through our approval
process. If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you
shortly to advise you. In such cases, the normal analysis, review
and approval process will be followed.

You should be aware that your letter and our response are
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure.

Sincerely,

[signature]

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

KED:plh:confadv1

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660
